title: Agents vs Chatbots
concept: Why an agent is goal-driven and acts autonomously, whereas a traditional chatbot is conversational and reactive.

exercises.prompt: In the last lesson, what are the three steps of an agent's core loop?
exercises.explain: An agent Perceives its environment, Reasons about what's going on, then Acts to change it. That loop is what lets an agent get things done on its own.

cards.title: Two Helpers, Two Styles
cards.body: Ask a chatbot 'What's a good pizza place?' and it tells you one. Ask an agent, and it might find the place, check the menu, and book a table. One talks, one acts.

cards.title: Agent vs Chatbot
cards.body: A chatbot is conversational and reactive: it responds when you speak. An agent is goal-driven and autonomous: give it a goal and it takes multiple steps by itself to reach it.

cards.title: Waiter vs Personal Chef
cards.body: A chatbot is like a waiter who answers your questions about the menu. An agent is like a personal chef: you say 'make me dinner,' and they plan, shop, and cook on their own.

cards.title: Booking a Trip
cards.body: Chatbot: 'Flights to Rome cost around $500.' Agent: given the goal 'book my Rome trip,' it compares flights, picks one, reserves a hotel, and adds it to your calendar.

cards.title: Myth: Agents Are Just Smarter Chatbots
cards.body: Not quite! The real difference isn't being 'smarter.' It's that an agent pursues a goal across many steps on its own, while a chatbot waits for each new message from you.

cards.title: Reactive vs Goal-Driven
cards.body: A chatbot reacts to each message. An agent chases a goal until it's done.

left.text: Reactive: answers, then waits for you to speak again.

right.text: Autonomous: keeps taking steps until the goal is reached.

exercises.prompt: Which best describes how an AI agent is different from a chatbot?

options.text: An agent is goal-driven and acts on its own across multiple steps.
An agent only replies with one message and then stops.
An agent can never use tools or take actions.
An agent waits silently until you ask it a question.

exercises.explain: The key trait of an agent is that it's goal-driven and autonomous: once given a goal, it acts step by step on its own instead of just replying and stopping.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A traditional chatbot is mainly reactive, responding to your messages rather than pursuing a goal by itself.
exercises.explain: True! A chatbot reacts to whatever you send and then waits. It doesn't chase a goal across many steps the way an agent does.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank about what makes an agent special.
exercises.sentence: Unlike a chatbot, an agent is goal-driven and acts autonomously to reach its target.
exercises.explain: An agent acts autonomously, meaning it takes steps on its own toward a goal instead of just replying to each message.

exercises.prompt: Which response sounds like an autonomous AI agent (not a simple chatbot reply)?

options.text: Goal received: I found 3 flights, booked the cheapest, reserved a hotel, and added both to your calendar.
Flights to Rome usually cost around $500. Anything else?
Sure, here's a fun fact about Rome's history!

exercises.explain: The first reply shows agent behavior: it took a goal and completed several actions on its own. The others just respond once and wait, like a chatbot.

exercises.prompt: Your friend says, 'Plan my birthday party.' A chatbot and an agent both help. Which pairing correctly matches each one's behavior?

options.text: Chatbot: suggests party ideas when asked. Agent: books the venue, orders a cake, and sends invites on its own.
Chatbot: books the venue and orders cake by itself. Agent: only lists ideas and waits.
Both do exactly the same thing with no difference.
Chatbot: acts across many steps autonomously. Agent: replies once then stops.

exercises.explain: A chatbot is reactive and conversational, giving answers when asked. An agent is goal-driven and autonomous, taking many actions on its own to reach the goal. Remembering this difference helps you know when you need a helper that talks versus one that gets things done.
